claim: A harness is everything that connects a model to the real world: skills, memory, system prompt, tools, sub-agents, and context that enables agents to complete complex tasks
actionable: When building agents, explicitly design the harness components (skills, memory, tools) rather than relying solely on the model's capabilities

claim: Deep Agents requires five core agent capabilities: execution environment with file system, context management, delegation with sub-agents, steering with human-in-the-loop, and durable execution with checkpointing
actionable: Implement these five capabilities in production agent systems to handle complex, long-running workflows reliably

claim: Sub-agents with isolated context solve three problems: they manage context by starting fresh, enable parallelization of work, and allow matching model capability to task complexity
actionable: Use sub-agents to decompose complex tasks and run them in parallel while maintaining clean context boundaries

claim: Progressive disclosure is a context management technique where agents load minimal skill information upfront and dynamically pull full skill resources only when needed
actionable: Implement progressive disclosure by loading skill summaries in the system prompt and fetching full documentation on demand

claim: Durable execution with checkpointing allows agents to resume from any failed step rather than restarting, and enables human-in-the-loop workflows that await human input indefinitely
actionable: Use checkpoint systems to enable fault tolerance and human approval workflows in production agents
